body:
![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

The method `send_success_response` is not defined on the `Handler` class (only `send_json_response` and `send_error_response` are available). Calling this will result in an `AttributeError` at runtime. It should be replaced with `send_json_response`.

```suggestion
            self.send_json_response({"status": "telemetry_accepted"})
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Since `send_success_response` is being replaced with `send_json_response` in `serve_dashboard.py`, this test assertion needs to be updated to check for `send_json_response` instead.

```suggestion
    mock_handler.send_json_response.assert_called_with(
        {"status": "telemetry_accepted"}
    )
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

There is a duplicate shebang `#!/usr/bin/env python3` on line 4. Please remove the duplicate shebang and clean up the imports.

```suggestion
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import time
```

body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Running synchronous `subprocess.run` calls with a 30-second timeout inside the request handler of a single-threaded blocking server (`socketserver.TCPServer`) is highly inefficient. Since the frontend polls `/api/bounties` every 5 seconds, any delay or timeout in the Jira API will block the entire server, making the dashboard completely unresponsive. Consider reducing the timeout significantly (e.g., to 5 seconds) or caching the bounty results asynchronously.
